title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The Mystery of the Deep Sea
content.text: The ocean depths remain one of the last (0) frontiers on Earth, with much of the seabed still unexplored. Despite the harsh conditions, including extreme pressure and total darkness, life (1) in these remote environments. Scientists have discovered extraordinary creatures that have (2) unique biological traits to survive. For instance, many deep-sea fish possess bioluminescent organs that (3) them to attract prey or find mates in the pitch-black water. The discovery of hydrothermal vents has further (4) our understanding of biology, showing that life can exist without sunlight by relying on chemical energy. Exploring these areas is a (5) task, requiring advanced submersibles and remote-controlled vehicles that can withstand the immense weight of the water above. Every mission into the deep (6) new species and geological features, providing valuable data about the history of our planet. As technology (7) , we are beginning to map the ocean floor with greater precision than ever before. This knowledge is (8) for protecting marine biodiversity and managing the resources of the deep sea sustainably, ensuring that this fragile and mysterious world is preserved for the benefit of future generations and the health of the global ecosystem.

example.explanation: “Frontiers” is used here to describe an area that is still unknown or unexplored.

questions.explanation: “Thrives” means to grow or develop well, especially in challenging environments.
“Evolved traits” is the correct biological term for developing characteristics over time.
“Enable them to [verb]” is a formal way to express making something possible.
“Broadened our understanding” is a common academic collocation.
“Daunting task” describes a job that is intimidating or very difficult to accomplish.
“Uncovers” is used when something new or hidden is discovered during an investigation.
“Technology advances” is the standard way to describe technological progress.
“Indispensable” means absolutely necessary and is used here for conservation efforts.
